examples.title: Pseudocode: Color Smoothing
examples.description: Component-wise color smoothing using a 3x3 averaging filter
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ColorSmoothing
INPUT: RGB image
OUTPUT: Smoothed RGB image

BEGIN
 FOR each pixel location (x, y) DO
 FOR each color channel (R, G, B) DO
 Compute average of 3x3 neighborhood
 Assign average value to output pixel
 END FOR
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates component-wise color smoothing using a 3x3 averaging filter. It iterates over each pixel location and color channel, computing the average of the 3x3 neighborhood and assigning the result to the output pixel.

examples.title: Python: Color Smoothing
examples.description: Component-wise color smoothing using a 3x3 averaging filter
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

# Load RGB image
img = Image.open('image.jpg')
img_array = np.array(img)

# Define 3x3 averaging filter
filter = np.ones((3, 3)) / 9

# Apply filter to each color channel
smoothed_img = np.zeros_like(img_array)
for i in range(3):
 smoothed_img[:, :, i] = np.convolve2d(img_array[:, :, i], filter, mode='same')

# Save smoothed image
smoothed_img = Image.fromarray(smoothed_img.astype(np.uint8))
smoothed_img.save('smoothed_image.jpg')
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates component-wise color smoothing using a 3x3 averaging filter. It loads an RGB image, defines the filter, applies it to each color channel using convolution, and saves the smoothed image.

examples.title: C: Color Sharpening
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates unsharp masking for color sharpening. It defines a function to apply unsharp masking to an RGB image, which computes a blurred version of the image and then adds a weighted difference between the original and blurred images to the original image.
